content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

## plugin:context7-plugin:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

- brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- dioxus: Dioxus 0.7 reactive UI framework reference (covers dioxus, dioxus-hooks, dioxus-signals, dioxus-stores, dioxus-router, dioxus-fullstack, dioxus-document). Use when writing Dioxus components, signals, effects, resources, server functions, stores, routing, rsx macro code, or fullstack/SSR apps. Also use when the user mentions Dioxus, asks about reactive patterns in Dioxus, or works on any .rs file that imports from dioxus::prelude::*. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — Dioxus APIs change frequently between versions and training data is often wrong.
- icu4x: ICU4X 2.x internationalization crate family reference (covers icu_calendar, icu_datetime, icu_plurals, icu_decimal, icu_collator, icu_segmenter, icu_locale, icu_provider, icu_experimental, fixed_decimal). Use when writing or reviewing any Rust code that uses ICU4X crates for i18n, l10n, date/time formatting, number formatting, plural rules, collation, text segmentation, or locale handling. Also use when the user mentions ICU4X, works on any .rs file that imports from icu::* or icu_*::*, or reviews spec files referencing ICU4X APIs. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — ICU4X had a major breaking redesign from 1.x to 2.x and training data is almost certainly wrong.
- leptos: Leptos 0.8 reactive web framework reference (covers leptos, leptos_router, reactive_stores, server_fn, leptos_meta). Use when writing Leptos components, signals, effects, resources, actions, server functions, stores, routing, view macro code, or SSR/hydration. Also use when the user mentions Leptos, asks about reactive patterns in Leptos, or works on any .rs file that imports from leptos::*. Even if the question seems simple, consult this skill — Leptos APIs change frequently and training data is often wrong.
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- post-implementation-audit: MANDATORY after every implementation task — runs three sequential audits on the new code (spec/impl drift, iterative "anything missing?", test coverage) and lands every finding in the same PR before the user-review step. Invoke as soon as an implementation task's named tests pass, OR when any of these phrases appears: "ready to present", "implementation is complete", "named tests pass", "before opening the PR", "ready for review". Skipping this audit leaves spec drift, untested defensive code, missing conventions, and silent contract violations in the merged PR.
- waiting-for-codex-review: Use immediately after pushing a PR to GitHub when Codex is configured to review the branch. Codex reviews asynchronously — it leaves a 👀 reaction on the PR while reviewing, drops the 👀 when the run finishes (either replacing it with 👍 if satisfied, or removing it and posting review threads if changes are needed). This skill defines the polling loop and the response workflow: wait for Codex's verdict, address any review threads it posts with the same rigor as `post-implementation-audit` (TDD + no coverage regression), commit, push, reply inline, resolve the thread, then keep waiting until 👍. Triggers on phrases like "wait for codex review", "what does codex say", "is codex done", "address the codex feedback", "check the PR reviews", or any post-push handoff where the user expects an automated reviewer's verdict. Invoke this skill even when the user does not explicitly name Codex — any GitHub PR where a 👀/👍 reaction signals an automated reviewer follows the same pattern.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

Keywords: create rust skill, create crate skill, create std skill, 创建 rust skill, 创建 crate skill, 创建 std skill, 动态 rust skill, 动态 crate skill, skill for tokio, skill for serde, skill for axum, generate rust skill, rust 技能, crate 技能, 从文档创建skill, from docs create skill

- rust-skills:rust-trait-explorer: Explore Rust trait implementations using LSP. Triggers on: /trait-impl, find implementations, who implements, trait 实现, 谁实现了, 实现了哪些trait
- rust-skills:unsafe-checker: CRITICAL: Use for unsafe Rust code review and FFI. Triggers on: unsafe, raw pointer, FFI, extern, transmute, *mut, *const, union, #[repr(C)], libc, std::ffi, MaybeUninit, NonNull, SAFETY comment, soundness, undefined behavior, UB, safe wrapper, memory layout, bindgen, cbindgen, CString, CStr, 安全抽象, 裸指针, 外部函数接口, 内存布局, 不安全代码, FFI 绑定, 未定义行为
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |

| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis

- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

{"assignees":[],"body":"**Epic: #224 (Agnostic date-time components)**

## Goal
Stateful composition of two DateField instances (start/end) with range coordination.

## Out of scope
Framework-specific adapter components (Leptos/Dioxus).

## Spec refs
- `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md`
- `spec/shared/date-time-types.md`

## Depends on
- #262 (DateField agnostic core)

## Layer
Component

## Framework
None

## Test tier
Unit

## Fibonacci points
3

## Element/ref handling note

DateRangeField agnostic code should own segment values, range validation, segment focus intent, keyboard editing, and ARIA/data attrs. Live segment/input focus, selection/caret behavior, and focus restoration must be adapter-resolved with native handles keyed by logical segment.

Stable string IDs remain valid for semantic ARIA relationships, hydration-stable markup, and `data-ars-*` hooks. They should not be used as a substitute for live element handles. Leptos adapters should prefer `NodeRef`/resolved DOM elements for live operations; Dioxus adapters should prefer `MountedData` or the Dioxus platform layer where available.

## Tests to add first
- Start and end date fields are both present.
- Start date cannot exceed end date (validation error on invalid range).
- Tab navigates from start segments to end segments sequentially.
- ConnectApi: group produces `role="group"`, with two sub-groups for start and end.
- Range separator element is present between start and end fields.
- Form integration with two hidden inputs (start and end ISO dates).
- Min/max constraints applied to both start and end fields.

## Acceptance criteria
- All public types documented per workspace `missing_docs` lint.
- State machine matches spec §1 exactly.
- ConnectApi produces correct ARIA attributes per spec §2.
- Snapshot tests cover the component's `connect()` / `Api` `AttrMap` output for every anatomy part and each output-affecting state, prop, or context branch, with committed `.snap` fixtures passing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.
- All named tests pass with zero warnings.

## Spec impact
No spec change required.

## Board update rule
If blocked, keep `Status` in `Todo`, use native GitHub issue dependencies, and name the dependency or spec ambiguity.

content.content:
# Spec Directory Guide

## File Conventions

- `_category.md` files contain category overviews and shared conventions
- Component files use kebab-case matching the component name
- Shared types in `shared/` are referenced by component frontmatter
- Headings are numbered (`## 1. State Machine`, `### 1.1 States`)

## Spec Changes During Implementation

The repo is in active implementation. Spec edits inside `spec/` should usually be tied to a concrete GitHub implementation task or PR, not made as detached cleanup.

- Read the implementation issue before changing spec text.
- Keep cited spec sections synchronized with the implementation task that discovered the mismatch.
- If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec instead of documenting it only in framework code.
- Do not leave placeholder notes for later reconciliation; resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task whenever possible.

## Adding a New Component

1. Create `components/{category}/{component-name}.md` with YAML frontmatter
2. Follow the section structure defined in `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the appropriate tier
3. Add an entry to `manifest.toml` with path, category, deps, and related
4. Update `foundation/02-component-catalog.md` with the new component
5. Run `cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate` to verify consistency

## Component File Format

```markdown
---
component: ComponentName
category: { category }
tier: stateless | stateful | complex
foundation_deps: [architecture, accessibility, ...]
shared_deps: [date-time-types, ...]
related: [sibling-component, ...]
---

# ComponentName
```

See `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the canonical section structure, ordering rules, conformance checklist, and skeleton examples for each tier.

2. Is there any existing component that COMPOSES child component Props (i.e., builds another component's `Props` struct and delegates via `ConnectApi`)? pub struct Props {

/// Inclusive date range.
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct DateRange {
    /// The inclusive range start.
    pub start: CalendarDate,

    /// The inclusive range end.
    pub end: CalendarDate,
}
```

**Methods:**
- `DateRange::new(start: CalendarDate, end: CalendarDate) -> Option<Self>` — Creates a range when `start <= end` within the same comparable calendar. Returns `None` if start > end or calendars/eras don't match.
- `DateRange::normalized(first_date: CalendarDate, second_date: CalendarDate) -> Option<Self>` — Creates a valid range by automatically ordering the two inputs. Swaps them if needed so the earliest date becomes `start`. Returns `None` if calendars/eras don't match.

- `day: u8` — 1-based day of month

/// Inclusive date range.
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct DateRange {
    /// The inclusive range start.
    pub start: CalendarDate,

    /// The inclusive range end.
    pub end: CalendarDate,
}
```

**Methods:**
- `DateRange::new(start: CalendarDate, end: CalendarDate) -> Option<Self>` — Creates a range when `start <= end` within the same comparable calendar. Returns `None` if start > end or calendars/eras don't match.
- `DateRange::normalized(first_date: CalendarDate, second_date: CalendarDate) -> Option<Self>` — Creates a valid range by automatically ordering the two inputs. Swaps them if needed so the earliest date becomes `start`. Returns `None` if calendars/eras don't match.

content.text: I now have a complete picture. I found several spec/code mismatches I'll resolve in-task, but one is a genuine design ambiguity worth confirming: the issue calls for a "validation error on invalid range," while the spec's state machine *normalizes* (swaps) start/end via `DateRange::normalized` so `start > end` can never persist.

questions.question: The spec machine normalizes start/end (swaps so start<=end always holds), but the issue's test says 'Start date cannot exceed end date (validation error on invalid range)' and spec prose §3.1/§5 call for a `data-ars-invalid` on Root that the spec's Api code never actually emits. How should the agnostic core treat range validity?

options.label: Normalize + invalid flag (recommended)
options.description: Keep the spec's swap-normalization so start<=end is a structural invariant, AND derive an `invalid` state from min/max bound violations exposed as `data-ars-invalid` on Root + an `is_invalid()` Api getter. The 'invalid range' test asserts (a) start>end input is swapped to a valid ordering, and (b) a range outside [min,max] surfaces data-ars-invalid. Resolves the spec's prose/code gap; keeps spec's DateRange::normalized design.

options.label: Flag start>end as invalid (no swap)
options.description: Change the machine to stop swapping: store the raw start/end and set data-ars-invalid when start>end (and on min/max violations). The 'invalid range' test asserts data-ars-invalid appears when start>end. This contradicts the spec's explicit use of DateRange::normalized and would require rewriting §1.6 transitions away from normalization.

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "The spec machine normalizes start/end (swaps so start<=end always holds), but the issue's test says 'Start date cannot exceed end date (validation error on invalid range)' and spec prose §3.1/§5 call for a `data-ars-invalid` on Root that the spec's Api code never actually emits. How should the agnostic core treat range validity?"="Normalize + invalid flag (recommended)". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

content.text: Decision recorded. Let me write the final plan.

input.content:
# Task #284 — Implement DateRangeField agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #284 (epic #224, depends on #262 DateField which is **closed/merged**) asks for the
framework-agnostic core of `DateRangeField`: a stateful composition of two `DateField` instances
(start/end) with shared range coordination, validation, and form integration. No adapter
(Leptos/Dioxus) work is in scope.

The component owns: segment value coordination across two child fields, range normalization,
min/max coordination between the fields, focus-field tracking (start vs end), ARIA/`data-ars-*`
attrs (`role="group"` with labelled sub-groups), a visual separator, and hidden form inputs
(combined ISO interval and/or separate start/end names).

Spec: `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` (tier: stateful).
Implementation lives in `ars-components`, mirroring the existing `date_field`, `range_calendar`,
and `date_picker` conventions.

## Spec/code mismatches to resolve in this PR

The spec's code examples were written against APIs that drifted. Per the spec-sync rule
(CLAUDE.md) these are fixed in `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` **in this PR**, and
the implementation follows the corrected contract:

1. **`date_field::Props` field names** — spec `start_field_props`/`end_field_props` use `min`/`max`;
   the real [`date_field::Props`](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_field/mod.rs#L101) uses
   `min_value`/`max_value`. Map accordingly.
2. **`DateRange::normalized` / `DateRange::new` return `Option<Self>`** (see
   [`helpers.rs`](crates/ars-i18n/src/calendar/helpers.rs#L308)) — spec does
   `ctx.value.set(Some(DateRange::normalized(..)))` which is a type error. Use
   `if let Some(range) = DateRange::normalized(start, end) { ctx.value.set(Some(range)) }`.
3. **`range_description` references nonexistent `self.ctx.format` and a nonexistent free
   `format_date`** — replace with locale-aware formatting via an `IntlBackend`, mirroring
   `range_calendar::format_date_label` ([range_calendar/mod.rs:2133](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/range_calendar/mod.rs#L2133)).
   This requires adding `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>` to `Context` (DateField/RangeCalendar
   both carry it; resolved from `env.intl_backend` in `init`).
4. **`format_iso(...)` free fn does not exist** — use `CalendarDate::to_iso8601()` and
   `DateRange::to_iso8601()` (which already emits `start/end`).
5. **`ActiveField` does not exist** (DateRangePicker not yet implemented) — define
   `pub enum ActiveField { Start, End }` in the `date_range_field` module, documented, so a later
   DateRangePicker task imports it. Update the spec comment accordingly.
6. **`CalendarDate::new_gregorian` takes `u8` and returns `Result`** (not `NonZeroU8`/`nzu8`) —
   the spec appendix `nzu8(..)` test snippets are corrected to the `date(y,m,d)` helper pattern
   used across the date-time test suites.
7. **Validation gap** — spec §3.1/§5 promise `data-ars-invalid` on Root, but the spec `Context`
   has no `invalid` field and `root_attrs` never emits it. **Resolution (user-confirmed):
   normalize + invalid flag.** Keep `DateRange::normalized` (start<=end is a structural invariant),
   and additionally derive an `invalid` state from min/max bound violations, surfaced as
   `data-ars-invalid` on Root and an `is_invalid()` Api getter. Add the derivation to spec §1.7.
8. **Min/max applied to both fields** — refine the child-prop coordination so global bounds always
   reach both fields while preserving cross-field coordination:
   - start: `min_value = ctx.min`, `max_value = end-if-range-set else ctx.max`
   - end:   `min_value = start-if-range-set else ctx.min`, `max_value = ctx.max`
   Update spec §1.7 to match.

## Module layout (new)

- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/mod.rs` — `State`, `Event`, `Context`,
  `Props`, `ActiveField`, `Machine`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. `type Effect = NoEffect`,
  `type Api<'a> = Api<'a>`.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/tests.rs` — unit + snapshot tests
  (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` from `mod.rs`).
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/snapshots/` — committed `.snap` insta
  fixtures.
- Register `pub mod date_range_field;` in
  [`date_time/mod.rs`](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs).

## Implementation (TDD: tests first)

Follow the `date_field`/`date_picker` test scaffolding exactly (helpers `date()`, `props()`,
`env()`, `service()`, `snapshot_attrs()`, `attr()` — see
[date_picker/tests.rs:1-75](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/tests.rs)).
`Env::new(locale, Arc::new(StubIntlBackend))`; `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &env, &Messages::default())`.

### State machine (spec §1.1–1.6)
- States `Idle | StartFocused | EndFocused`; Events `FocusStart | FocusEnd | BlurAll |
  SetRange(Option<DateRange>) | StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>) | EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`.
- `Context`: `value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>`, `active_field: Option<ActiveField>`,
  `min`/`max: Option<CalendarDate>`, `locale`, `intl_backend` (added), `messages`, `disabled`,
  `readonly`, `required`, `name`/`start_name`/`end_name`, `ids: ComponentIds`,
  `has_description`/`has_error_message`, `force_leading_zeros`. Manual `Debug` (backend is `dyn`).
- `transition`: guard `is_disabled` returns `None`; focus events set `active_field` + state; blur
  resets; `SetRange` sets value; `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange` coordinate + normalize via
  `DateRange::normalized(..)` (the `Option` is matched, not unwrapped).
- Document every public item (`missing_docs`). Use `.expect("reason")`, `#[expect(..)]` not
  `#[allow]`, descriptive names, owned strings into `set()`.

### Connect / Api (spec §1.7)
- `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "date-range-field"] enum Part { Root, Label, StartField,
  Separator, EndField, Description, ErrorMessage, HiddenInput }`.
- `root_attrs`: scope/part data attrs, id, `role="group"`, `aria-labelledby`→label, `data-ars-state`,
  `aria-describedby` (description + error-message when present), `data-ars-disabled`,
  `data-ars-readonly`, `aria-required`, and **`data-ars-invalid` when `is_invalid()`**.
- `label_attrs`, `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`), `separator_text()`, `description_attrs`,
  `error_message_attrs` (`role="alert"`), `hidden_input_attrs` (combined `start/end` ISO via
  `DateRange::to_iso8601`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs` (per
  `start_name`/`end_name`, value via `CalendarDate::to_iso8601`).
- `start_field_props()`/`end_field_props()` → `date_field::Props` with corrected `min_value`/
  `max_value` coordination (mismatch #8), `aria_label` from messages, `force_leading_zeros` passed
  through. `part_attrs` delegates `StartField`/`EndField` via `.into()` on the child Props.
- `range_description()` → `Option<String>` using `intl_backend` + a local `format_date_label`-style
  helper and the `range_description` message.
- `selected_range()`, `is_focused()`, `is_invalid()`, `state_name()`.

### Messages (spec §4.1)
`start_label`, `end_label`, `separator_text`, `range_description` with the spec defaults;
`impl ComponentMessages for Messages`.

## Tests to add (issue "Tests to add first" + acceptance criteria)

Unit/connect tests in `tests.rs`:
- Both start and end field props are produced (`StartField`/`EndField` parts present).
- Setting start>end normalizes to start<=end (invariant holds); a range outside `[min,max]`
  surfaces `data-ars-invalid` + `is_invalid()` true.
- Focus tracking: `FocusStart`→`StartFocused`, `FocusEnd`→`EndFocused`, `BlurAll`→`Idle`
  (Tab-sequencing is adapter-resolved; assert the focus-state machine here).
- `root_attrs` → `role="group"`; start/end field props carry their own `role="group"` sub-grouping
  (via `date_field` group attrs) with distinct ids/aria-labels.
- Separator part present with `aria-hidden` + `separator_text`.
- Form: combined hidden input ISO `start/end`; separate `start_name`/`end_name` hidden inputs.
- Min/max applied to both fields (assert `min_value`/`max_value` on both child props).
- `disabled` ignores events; required sets `aria-required`.

Snapshot tests (`snapshot_*`, `insta::assert_snapshot!`): `root_attrs` for each output-affecting
state/branch (idle, start-focused, end-focused, disabled/readonly, required, invalid,
with-description/error), label, separator, description, error-message, hidden-input (empty + set),
start/end hidden inputs, and start/end field props. Commit `.snap` fixtures; verify with
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

[tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs](crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs)
via `assert_anatomy`, listing all 8 parts.

Proptest: add a state-machine block to
[tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs](crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs)
asserting invariants (e.g. stored range always has start<=end after arbitrary
SetRange/StartValueChange/EndValueChange sequences; disabled never transitions). Use
`super::common::proptest_config()`.

## Post-implementation audit (MANDATORY)

After named tests pass, invoke the `post-implementation-audit` skill: (a) spec/impl drift, (b) ≥2
"anything missing?" passes, (c) test-coverage audit. Land every finding in this PR. Confirm the
spec edits (mismatches #1–#8) are consistent end-to-end (Context, transitions, Api, anatomy table,
§3/§5 prose, appendix tests).

## Verification

```bash
# Focused during development
cargo test -p ars-components date_range_field
cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance date_range_field
cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines date_time
cargo insta test -p ars-components --review            # then accept intentional snapshots
cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject

# Pre-push gate (after user approval), per CLAUDE.md step 9
cargo xci-fast      # fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, snapshot-count, native coverage
```

Zero warnings required. Then: present for user review → on approval `cargo xci-fast` → commit +
push PR to `main` with `Closes #284` → add `snapshot-reviewed` label (new `.snap` fixtures) →
invoke `waiting-for-codex-review` skill and loop until 👍 → merge → close issue.

# Task #284 — Implement DateRangeField agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #284 (epic #224, depends on #262 DateField which is **closed/merged**) asks for the
framework-agnostic core of `DateRangeField`: a stateful composition of two `DateField` instances
(start/end) with shared range coordination, validation, and form integration. No adapter
(Leptos/Dioxus) work is in scope.

The component owns: segment value coordination across two child fields, range normalization,
min/max coordination between the fields, focus-field tracking (start vs end), ARIA/`data-ars-*`
attrs (`role="group"` with labelled sub-groups), a visual separator, and hidden form inputs
(combined ISO interval and/or separate start/end names).

Spec: `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` (tier: stateful).
Implementation lives in `ars-components`, mirroring the existing `date_field`, `range_calendar`,
and `date_picker` conventions.

## Spec/code mismatches to resolve in this PR

The spec's code examples were written against APIs that drifted. Per the spec-sync rule
(CLAUDE.md) these are fixed in `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` **in this PR**, and
the implementation follows the corrected contract:

1. **`date_field::Props` field names** — spec `start_field_props`/`end_field_props` use `min`/`max`;
   the real [`date_field::Props`](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_field/mod.rs#L101) uses
   `min_value`/`max_value`. Map accordingly.
2. **`DateRange::normalized` / `DateRange::new` return `Option<Self>`** (see
   [`helpers.rs`](crates/ars-i18n/src/calendar/helpers.rs#L308)) — spec does
   `ctx.value.set(Some(DateRange::normalized(..)))` which is a type error. Use
   `if let Some(range) = DateRange::normalized(start, end) { ctx.value.set(Some(range)) }`.
3. **`range_description` references nonexistent `self.ctx.format` and a nonexistent free
   `format_date`** — replace with locale-aware formatting via an `IntlBackend`, mirroring
   `range_calendar::format_date_label` ([range_calendar/mod.rs:2133](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/range_calendar/mod.rs#L2133)).
   This requires adding `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>` to `Context` (DateField/RangeCalendar
   both carry it; resolved from `env.intl_backend` in `init`).
4. **`format_iso(...)` free fn does not exist** — use `CalendarDate::to_iso8601()` and
   `DateRange::to_iso8601()` (which already emits `start/end`).
5. **`ActiveField` does not exist** (DateRangePicker not yet implemented) — define
   `pub enum ActiveField { Start, End }` in the `date_range_field` module, documented, so a later
   DateRangePicker task imports it. Update the spec comment accordingly.
6. **`CalendarDate::new_gregorian` takes `u8` and returns `Result`** (not `NonZeroU8`/`nzu8`) —
   the spec appendix `nzu8(..)` test snippets are corrected to the `date(y,m,d)` helper pattern
   used across the date-time test suites.
7. **Validation gap** — spec §3.1/§5 promise `data-ars-invalid` on Root, but the spec `Context`
   has no `invalid` field and `root_attrs` never emits it. **Resolution (user-confirmed):
   normalize + invalid flag.** Keep `DateRange::normalized` (start<=end is a structural invariant),
   and additionally derive an `invalid` state from min/max bound violations, surfaced as
   `data-ars-invalid` on Root and an `is_invalid()` Api getter. Add the derivation to spec §1.7.
8. **Min/max applied to both fields** — refine the child-prop coordination so global bounds always
   reach both fields while preserving cross-field coordination:
   - start: `min_value = ctx.min`, `max_value = end-if-range-set else ctx.max`
   - end:   `min_value = start-if-range-set else ctx.min`, `max_value = ctx.max`
   Update spec §1.7 to match.

## Module layout (new)

- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/mod.rs` — `State`, `Event`, `Context`,
  `Props`, `ActiveField`, `Machine`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. `type Effect = NoEffect`,
  `type Api<'a> = Api<'a>`.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/tests.rs` — unit + snapshot tests
  (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` from `mod.rs`).
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/snapshots/` — committed `.snap` insta
  fixtures.
- Register `pub mod date_range_field;` in
  [`date_time/mod.rs`](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs).

## Implementation (TDD: tests first)

Follow the `date_field`/`date_picker` test scaffolding exactly (helpers `date()`, `props()`,
`env()`, `service()`, `snapshot_attrs()`, `attr()` — see
[date_picker/tests.rs:1-75](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/tests.rs)).
`Env::new(locale, Arc::new(StubIntlBackend))`; `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &env, &Messages::default())`.

### State machine (spec §1.1–1.6)
- States `Idle | StartFocused | EndFocused`; Events `FocusStart | FocusEnd | BlurAll |
  SetRange(Option<DateRange>) | StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>) | EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`.
- `Context`: `value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>`, `active_field: Option<ActiveField>`,
  `min`/`max: Option<CalendarDate>`, `locale`, `intl_backend` (added), `messages`, `disabled`,
  `readonly`, `required`, `name`/`start_name`/`end_name`, `ids: ComponentIds`,
  `has_description`/`has_error_message`, `force_leading_zeros`. Manual `Debug` (backend is `dyn`).
- `transition`: guard `is_disabled` returns `None`; focus events set `active_field` + state; blur
  resets; `SetRange` sets value; `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange` coordinate + normalize via
  `DateRange::normalized(..)` (the `Option` is matched, not unwrapped).
- Document every public item (`missing_docs`). Use `.expect("reason")`, `#[expect(..)]` not
  `#[allow]`, descriptive names, owned strings into `set()`.

### Connect / Api (spec §1.7)
- `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "date-range-field"] enum Part { Root, Label, StartField,
  Separator, EndField, Description, ErrorMessage, HiddenInput }`.
- `root_attrs`: scope/part data attrs, id, `role="group"`, `aria-labelledby`→label, `data-ars-state`,
  `aria-describedby` (description + error-message when present), `data-ars-disabled`,
  `data-ars-readonly`, `aria-required`, and **`data-ars-invalid` when `is_invalid()`**.
- `label_attrs`, `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`), `separator_text()`, `description_attrs`,
  `error_message_attrs` (`role="alert"`), `hidden_input_attrs` (combined `start/end` ISO via
  `DateRange::to_iso8601`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs` (per
  `start_name`/`end_name`, value via `CalendarDate::to_iso8601`).
- `start_field_props()`/`end_field_props()` → `date_field::Props` with corrected `min_value`/
  `max_value` coordination (mismatch #8), `aria_label` from messages, `force_leading_zeros` passed
  through. `part_attrs` delegates `StartField`/`EndField` via `.into()` on the child Props.
- `range_description()` → `Option<String>` using `intl_backend` + a local `format_date_label`-style
  helper and the `range_description` message.
- `selected_range()`, `is_focused()`, `is_invalid()`, `state_name()`.

### Messages (spec §4.1)
`start_label`, `end_label`, `separator_text`, `range_description` with the spec defaults;
`impl ComponentMessages for Messages`.

## Tests to add (issue "Tests to add first" + acceptance criteria)

Unit/connect tests in `tests.rs`:
- Both start and end field props are produced (`StartField`/`EndField` parts present).

  surfaces `data-ars-invalid` + `is_invalid()` true.
- Focus tracking: `FocusStart`→`StartFocused`, `FocusEnd`→`EndFocused`, `BlurAll`→`Idle`
  (Tab-sequencing is adapter-resolved; assert the focus-state machine here).
- `root_attrs` → `role="group"`; start/end field props carry their own `role="group"` sub-grouping
  (via `date_field` group attrs) with distinct ids/aria-labels.
- Separator part present with `aria-hidden` + `separator_text`.
- Form: combined hidden input ISO `start/end`; separate `start_name`/`end_name` hidden inputs.
- Min/max applied to both fields (assert `min_value`/`max_value` on both child props).
- `disabled` ignores events; required sets `aria-required`.

Snapshot tests (`snapshot_*`, `insta::assert_snapshot!`): `root_attrs` for each output-affecting
state/branch (idle, start-focused, end-focused, disabled/readonly, required, invalid,
with-description/error), label, separator, description, error-message, hidden-input (empty + set),
start/end hidden inputs, and start/end field props. Commit `.snap` fixtures; verify with
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

[tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs](crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs)
via `assert_anatomy`, listing all 8 parts.

# Task #284 — Implement DateRangeField agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #284 (epic #224, depends on #262 DateField which is **closed/merged**) asks for the
framework-agnostic core of `DateRangeField`: a stateful composition of two `DateField` instances
(start/end) with shared range coordination, validation, and form integration. No adapter
(Leptos/Dioxus) work is in scope.

The component owns: segment value coordination across two child fields, range normalization,
min/max coordination between the fields, focus-field tracking (start vs end), ARIA/`data-ars-*`
attrs (`role="group"` with labelled sub-groups), a visual separator, and hidden form inputs
(combined ISO interval and/or separate start/end names).

Spec: `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` (tier: stateful).
Implementation lives in `ars-components`, mirroring the existing `date_field`, `range_calendar`,
and `date_picker` conventions.

## Spec/code mismatches to resolve in this PR

The spec's code examples were written against APIs that drifted. Per the spec-sync rule
(CLAUDE.md) these are fixed in `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md` **in this PR**, and
the implementation follows the corrected contract:

1. **`date_field::Props` field names** — spec `start_field_props`/`end_field_props` use `min`/`max`;
   the real [`date_field::Props`](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_field/mod.rs#L101) uses
   `min_value`/`max_value`. Map accordingly.
2. **`DateRange::normalized` / `DateRange::new` return `Option<Self>`** (see
   [`helpers.rs`](crates/ars-i18n/src/calendar/helpers.rs#L308)) — spec does
   `ctx.value.set(Some(DateRange::normalized(..)))` which is a type error. Use
   `if let Some(range) = DateRange::normalized(start, end) { ctx.value.set(Some(range)) }`.
3. **`range_description` references nonexistent `self.ctx.format` and a nonexistent free
   `format_date`** — replace with locale-aware formatting via an `IntlBackend`, mirroring
   `range_calendar::format_date_label` ([range_calendar/mod.rs:2133](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/range_calendar/mod.rs#L2133)).
   This requires adding `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>` to `Context` (DateField/RangeCalendar
   both carry it; resolved from `env.intl_backend` in `init`).
4. **`format_iso(...)` free fn does not exist** — use `CalendarDate::to_iso8601()` and
   `DateRange::to_iso8601()` (which already emits `start/end`).
5. **`ActiveField` does not exist** (DateRangePicker not yet implemented) — define
   `pub enum ActiveField { Start, End }` in the `date_range_field` module, documented, so a later
   DateRangePicker task imports it. Update the spec comment accordingly.
6. **`CalendarDate::new_gregorian` takes `u8` and returns `Result`** (not `NonZeroU8`/`nzu8`) —
   the spec appendix `nzu8(..)` test snippets are corrected to the `date(y,m,d)` helper pattern
   used across the date-time test suites.
7. **Validation gap** — spec §3.1/§5 promise `data-ars-invalid` on Root, but the spec `Context`
   has no `invalid` field and `root_attrs` never emits it. **Resolution (user-confirmed):
   normalize + invalid flag.** Keep `DateRange::normalized` (start<=end is a structural invariant),
   and additionally derive an `invalid` state from min/max bound violations, surfaced as
   `data-ars-invalid` on Root and an `is_invalid()` Api getter. Add the derivation to spec §1.7.
8. **Min/max applied to both fields** — refine the child-prop coordination so global bounds always
   reach both fields while preserving cross-field coordination:
   - start: `min_value = ctx.min`, `max_value = end-if-range-set else ctx.max`
   - end:   `min_value = start-if-range-set else ctx.min`, `max_value = ctx.max`
   Update spec §1.7 to match.

## Module layout (new)

- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/mod.rs` — `State`, `Event`, `Context`,
  `Props`, `ActiveField`, `Machine`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. `type Effect = NoEffect`,
  `type Api<'a> = Api<'a>`.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/tests.rs` — unit + snapshot tests
  (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` from `mod.rs`).
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/snapshots/` — committed `.snap` insta
  fixtures.
- Register `pub mod date_range_field;` in
  [`date_time/mod.rs`](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs).

## Implementation (TDD: tests first)

Follow the `date_field`/`date_picker` test scaffolding exactly (helpers `date()`, `props()`,
`env()`, `service()`, `snapshot_attrs()`, `attr()` — see
[date_picker/tests.rs:1-75](crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/tests.rs)).
`Env::new(locale, Arc::new(StubIntlBackend))`; `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &env, &Messages::default())`.

### State machine (spec §1.1–1.6)
- States `Idle | StartFocused | EndFocused`; Events `FocusStart | FocusEnd | BlurAll |
  SetRange(Option<DateRange>) | StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>) | EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`.
- `Context`: `value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>`, `active_field: Option<ActiveField>`,
  `min`/`max: Option<CalendarDate>`, `locale`, `intl_backend` (added), `messages`, `disabled`,
  `readonly`, `required`, `name`/`start_name`/`end_name`, `ids: ComponentIds`,
  `has_description`/`has_error_message`, `force_leading_zeros`. Manual `Debug` (backend is `dyn`).
- `transition`: guard `is_disabled` returns `None`; focus events set `active_field` + state; blur
  resets; `SetRange` sets value; `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange` coordinate + normalize via
  `DateRange::normalized(..)` (the `Option` is matched, not unwrapped).
- Document every public item (`missing_docs`). Use `.expect("reason")`, `#[expect(..)]` not
  `#[allow]`, descriptive names, owned strings into `set()`.

### Connect / Api (spec §1.7)
- `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "date-range-field"] enum Part { Root, Label, StartField,
  Separator, EndField, Description, ErrorMessage, HiddenInput }`.
- `root_attrs`: scope/part data attrs, id, `role="group"`, `aria-labelledby`→label, `data-ars-state`,
  `aria-describedby` (description + error-message when present), `data-ars-disabled`,
  `data-ars-readonly`, `aria-required`, and **`data-ars-invalid` when `is_invalid()`**.
- `label_attrs`, `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`), `separator_text()`, `description_attrs`,
  `error_message_attrs` (`role="alert"`), `hidden_input_attrs` (combined `start/end` ISO via
  `DateRange::to_iso8601`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs` (per
  `start_name`/`end_name`, value via `CalendarDate::to_iso8601`).
- `start_field_props()`/`end_field_props()` → `date_field::Props` with corrected `min_value`/
  `max_value` coordination (mismatch #8), `aria_label` from messages, `force_leading_zeros` passed
  through. `part_attrs` delegates `StartField`/`EndField` via `.into()` on the child Props.
- `range_description()` → `Option<String>` using `intl_backend` + a local `format_date_label`-style
  helper and the `range_description` message.
- `selected_range()`, `is_focused()`, `is_invalid()`, `state_name()`.

### Messages (spec §4.1)
`start_label`, `end_label`, `separator_text`, `range_description` with the spec defaults;
`impl ComponentMessages for Messages`.

## Tests to add (issue "Tests to add first" + acceptance criteria)

Unit/connect tests in `tests.rs`:
- Both start and end field props are produced (`StartField`/`EndField` parts present).
- Setting start>end normalizes to start<=end (invariant holds); a range outside `[min,max]`
  surfaces `data-ars-invalid` + `is_invalid()` true.
- Focus tracking: `FocusStart`→`StartFocused`, `FocusEnd`→`EndFocused`, `BlurAll`→`Idle`
  (Tab-sequencing is adapter-resolved; assert the focus-state machine here).
- `root_attrs` → `role="group"`; start/end field props carry their own `role="group"` sub-grouping
  (via `date_field` group attrs) with distinct ids/aria-labels.
- Separator part present with `aria-hidden` + `separator_text`.
- Form: combined hidden input ISO `start/end`; separate `start_name`/`end_name` hidden inputs.
- Min/max applied to both fields (assert `min_value`/`max_value` on both child props).
- `disabled` ignores events; required sets `aria-required`.

Snapshot tests (`snapshot_*`, `insta::assert_snapshot!`): `root_attrs` for each output-affecting
state/branch (idle, start-focused, end-focused, disabled/readonly, required, invalid,
with-description/error), label, separator, description, error-message, hidden-input (empty + set),
start/end hidden inputs, and start/end field props. Commit `.snap` fixtures; verify with
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`.

Spec-conformance: add `date_range_field_anatomy_matches_spec` to
[tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs](crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs)
via `assert_anatomy`, listing all 8 parts.

Proptest: add a state-machine block to
[tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs](crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs)
asserting invariants (e.g. stored range always has start<=end after arbitrary
SetRange/StartValueChange/EndValueChange sequences; disabled never transitions). Use
`super::common::proptest_config()`.

## Post-implementation audit (MANDATORY)

After named tests pass, invoke the `post-implementation-audit` skill: (a) spec/impl drift, (b) ≥2
"anything missing?" passes, (c) test-coverage audit. Land every finding in this PR. Confirm the
spec edits (mismatches #1–#8) are consistent end-to-end (Context, transitions, Api, anatomy table,
§3/§5 prose, appendix tests).

## Verification

```bash

cargo test -p ars-components date_range_field
cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance date_range_field
cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines date_time
cargo insta test -p ars-components --review            # then accept intentional snapshots
cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject

# Pre-push gate (after user approval), per CLAUDE.md step 9
cargo xci-fast      # fmt, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, snapshot-count, native coverage
```

1	//! DateRangeField component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! This module implements the framework-agnostic two-field date range input
4	//! defined in `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md`. It composes two
5	//! [`date_field`] instances (start and end) with shared range coordination,
6	//! min/max validation, focus-field tracking, and form integration. There is no
7	//! popover — the two fields render inline with a visual separator between them.
8	
9	#[cfg(test)]
10	mod tests;
11	
12	use alloc::{
13	    format,
14	    string::{String, ToString},
15	    sync::Arc,
16	    vec::Vec,
17	};
18	use core::{
19	    cmp::Ordering,
20	    fmt::{self, Debug},
21	};
22	
23	use ars_core::{
24	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env,
25	    HtmlAttr, IntlBackend, Locale, MessageFn, NoEffect, TransitionPlan,
26	};
27	use ars_i18n::{CalendarDate, DateRange};
28	
29	use super::date_field;
30	
31	/// Identifies which of the two child fields is currently active.
32	///
33	/// Owned by `DateRangeField` as the first range component to require it; later
34	/// range components (e.g. `DateRangePicker`) reuse this enum rather than
35	/// redefining it.
36	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
37	pub enum ActiveField {
38	    /// The start field is active.
39	    Start,
40	
41	    /// The end field is active.
42	    End,
43	}
44	
45	/// States for the `DateRangeField` component.
46	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
47	pub enum State {
48	    /// Neither field holds focus.
49	    Idle,
50	
51	    /// The start field holds focus.
52	    StartFocused,
53	
54	    /// The end field holds focus.
55	    EndFocused,
56	}
57	
58	/// Events for the `DateRangeField` component.
59	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
60	pub enum Event {
61	    /// Focus moved to the start field.
62	    FocusStart,
63	
64	    /// Focus moved to the end field.
65	    FocusEnd,
66	
67	    /// Focus left the entire component.
68	    BlurAll,
69	
70	    /// Set the full range programmatically.
71	    SetRange(Option<DateRange>),
72	
73	    /// The start field's value changed.
74	    StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>),
75	
76	    /// The end field's value changed.
77	    EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>),
78	}
79	
80	/// Context for the `DateRangeField` component.
81	#[derive(Clone)]
82	pub struct Context {
83	    /// Controlled or uncontrolled range value.
84	    pub value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>,
85	
86	    /// The active field, if either currently holds focus.
87	    pub active_field: Option<ActiveField>,
88	
89	    /// The minimum selectable date applied to both fields.
90	    pub min: Option<CalendarDate>,
91	
92	    /// The maximum selectable date applied to both fields.
93	    pub max: Option<CalendarDate>,
94	
95	    /// The resolved locale.
96	    pub locale: Locale,
97	
98	    /// Backend used for locale-dependent labels in range descriptions.
99	    pub intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>,
100	
101	    /// Resolved translatable messages.
102	    pub messages: Messages,
103	
104	    /// Whether the component is disabled.
105	    pub disabled: bool,
106	
107	    /// Whether the component is read-only.
108	    pub readonly: bool,
109	
110	    /// Whether the component is required for form submission.
111	    pub required: bool,
112	
113	    /// Form field name for a single hidden input carrying the range value.
114	    pub name: Option<String>,
115	
116	    /// Form field name for a separate hidden input carrying the start date.
117	    pub start_name: Option<String>,
118	
119	    /// Form field name for a separate hidden input carrying the end date.
120	    pub end_name: Option<String>,
121	
122	    /// Derived component part ids.
123	    pub ids: ComponentIds,
124	
125	    /// Whether the `Description` part is rendered.
126	    pub has_description: bool,
127	
128	    /// Whether the `ErrorMessage` part is rendered.
129	    pub has_error_message: bool,
130	
131	    /// When `true`, numeric segments in both child fields display with leading
132	    /// zeros (e.g. "03" instead of "3").
133	    pub force_leading_zeros: bool,
134	}

136	impl Debug for Context {
137	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
138	        f.debug_struct("Context")
139	            .field("value", &self.value)
140	            .field("active_field", &self.active_field)
141	            .field("min", &self.min)
142	            .field("max", &self.max)
143	            .field("locale", &self.locale)
144	            .field("intl_backend", &"<dyn IntlBackend>")
145	            .field("messages", &self.messages)
146	            .field("disabled", &self.disabled)
147	            .field("readonly", &self.readonly)
148	            .field("required", &self.required)
149	            .field("name", &self.name)
150	            .field("start_name", &self.start_name)
151	            .field("end_name", &self.end_name)
152	            .field("ids", &self.ids)
153	            .field("has_description", &self.has_description)
154	            .field("has_error_message", &self.has_error_message)
155	            .field("force_leading_zeros", &self.force_leading_zeros)
156	            .finish()
157	    }
158	}
159	
160	impl Context {
161	    /// Returns `true` when the current range violates the configured min/max
162	    /// bounds.
163	    ///
164	    /// The stored range always satisfies `start <= end` (it is normalized on
165	    /// every value change), so the only remaining source of invalidity is a
166	    /// start earlier than `min` or an end later than `max`.
167	    #[must_use]
168	    pub fn is_invalid(&self) -> bool {
169	        let Some(range) = self.value.get() else {
170	            return false;
171	        };
172	
173	        let below_min = self.min.as_ref().is_some_and(|min| {
174	            matches!(
175	                range.start.compare_within_calendar(min),
176	                Some(Ordering::Less)
177	            )
178	        });
179	
180	        let above_max = self.max.as_ref().is_some_and(|max| {
181	            matches!(
182	                range.end.compare_within_calendar(max),
183	                Some(Ordering::Greater)
184	            )
185	        });
186	
187	        below_min || above_max
188	    }
189	}

content.text: Found a real design gap (shared with the spec): the parent can't complete a range from two separate `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange` events because `Option<DateRange>` can't hold a partial (only-start) value. I'll track each field's value independently in Context and derive the complete range. Let me implement that.

+    /// assembled incrementally as each field changes.
+    /// The end field's current value, tracked independently so a range can be
+    /// assembled incrementally as each field changes.
